comment_text.sn35.94:4.1: This series of verses resembles those attributed to Vardhamāna (Mahāvīra) at Isibhāsiyāiṁ 29. The same series of verses also contains parallels to Snp 5.2:3.1, SN 1.6:3.1, and DN 2:29.4 = MN 56:12.2.
comment_text.sn35.94:8.3: The mention of “duality” links this to the preceding two discourses.
comment_text.sn35.94:9.2: “Attraction” (upāya, verb form upeti, here spelled upayanti) is the initial stage in the process of becoming attached (eg. SN 12.15:2.5).
comment_text.sn35.94:9.3: This unusual use of manomayaṁ (normally “mind-made”, here “mental construction”) parallels vitakkaṁ at Thag 21.1:6.2.

root_text.sn35.94:1.2: “Chayime, bhikkhave, phassāyatanā adantā aguttā arakkhitā asaṁvutā dukkhādhivāhā honti.
root_text.sn35.94:1.4: Cakkhu, bhikkhave, phassāyatanaṁ adantaṁ aguttaṁ arakkhitaṁ asaṁvutaṁ dukkhādhivāhaṁ hoti …pe…
root_text.sn35.94:1.5: jivhā, bhikkhave, phassāyatanaṁ adantaṁ aguttaṁ arakkhitaṁ asaṁvutaṁ dukkhādhivāhaṁ hoti …pe…
root_text.sn35.94:1.6: mano, bhikkhave, phassāyatanaṁ adantaṁ aguttaṁ arakkhitaṁ asaṁvutaṁ dukkhādhivāhaṁ hoti.
root_text.sn35.94:1.7: Ime kho, bhikkhave, cha phassāyatanā adantā aguttā arakkhitā asaṁvutā dukkhādhivāhā honti”.
root_text.sn35.94:2.1: Chayime, bhikkhave, phassāyatanā sudantā suguttā surakkhitā susaṁvutā sukhādhivāhā honti.
root_text.sn35.94:2.3: Cakkhu, bhikkhave, phassāyatanaṁ sudantaṁ suguttaṁ surakkhitaṁ susaṁvutaṁ sukhādhivāhaṁ hoti …pe…
root_text.sn35.94:2.4: jivhā, bhikkhave, phassāyatanaṁ sudantaṁ suguttaṁ surakkhitaṁ susaṁvutaṁ sukhādhivāhaṁ hoti …pe…
root_text.sn35.94:2.5: mano, bhikkhave, phassāyatanaṁ sudantaṁ suguttaṁ surakkhitaṁ susaṁvutaṁ sukhādhivāhaṁ hoti.
root_text.sn35.94:2.6: Ime kho, bhikkhave, cha phassāyatanā sudantā suguttā surakkhitā susaṁvutā sukhādhivāhā hontī”ti.

translation_text.sn35.94:1.2: “Mendicants, these six fields of contact bring suffering when they’re untamed, unguarded, unprotected, and unrestrained.
translation_text.sn35.94:1.4: The field of eye contact brings suffering when it’s untamed, unguarded, unprotected, and unrestrained.
translation_text.sn35.94:1.5: The field of ear contact … nose contact … tongue contact … body contact …
translation_text.sn35.94:1.6: The field of mind contact brings suffering when it’s untamed, unguarded, unprotected, and unrestrained.
translation_text.sn35.94:1.7: These six fields of contact bring suffering when they’re untamed, unguarded, unprotected, and unrestrained.
translation_text.sn35.94:2.1: These six fields of contact bring happiness when they’re well tamed, well guarded, well protected, and well restrained.
translation_text.sn35.94:2.3: The field of eye contact brings happiness when it’s well tamed, well guarded, well protected, and well restrained.
translation_text.sn35.94:2.4: The field of ear contact … nose contact … tongue contact … body contact …
translation_text.sn35.94:2.5: The field of mind contact brings happiness when it’s well tamed, well guarded, well protected, and well restrained.
translation_text.sn35.94:2.6: These six fields of contact bring happiness when they’re well tamed, well guarded, well protected, and well restrained.”
translation_text.sn35.94:2.7: That is what the Buddha said.
translation_text.sn35.94:2.8: Then the Holy One, the Teacher, went on to say:
translation_text.sn35.94:3.1: “Mendicants, it’s just the six fields of contact
translation_text.sn35.94:3.2: that lead the unrestrained to suffering.
translation_text.sn35.94:3.3: Those who understand how to restrain them
translation_text.sn35.94:3.4: live with faith as partner, not festering.
translation_text.sn35.94:4.3: get rid of all manner of desire for the pleasant,
translation_text.sn35.94:4.4: without hating what you don’t like.
translation_text.sn35.94:5.1: When you’ve heard sounds both liked and disliked,
translation_text.sn35.94:5.2: don’t fall under the thrall of sounds you like,
translation_text.sn35.94:5.3: get rid of hate for the unliked,
translation_text.sn35.94:5.4: and don’t hurt your mind <j>by thinking of what you don’t like.
translation_text.sn35.94:6.1: When you’ve smelled a pleasant, fragrant scent,
translation_text.sn35.94:6.2: and one that’s foul and unpleasant,
translation_text.sn35.94:6.3: get rid of repulsion for the unpleasant,
translation_text.sn35.94:6.4: while not yielding to desire for the pleasant.
translation_text.sn35.94:7.1: When you’ve enjoyed a sweet, delicious taste,
translation_text.sn35.94:7.2: and sometimes those that are bitter,
translation_text.sn35.94:7.3: don’t be attached to enjoying sweet tastes,
translation_text.sn35.94:8.1: Don’t be intoxicated by a pleasant touch,
translation_text.sn35.94:8.2: and don’t tremble at a painful touch.
translation_text.sn35.94:8.3: Look with equanimity at the duality <j>of pleasant and painful contacts,
translation_text.sn35.94:9.1: People generally let their perceptions proliferate;
translation_text.sn35.94:9.2: perceiving and proliferating, they are attracted.
translation_text.sn35.94:9.3: Casting aside all mental constructions of the domestic life,
translation_text.sn35.94:10.1: When the mind is well developed like this <j>regarding the six,
translation_text.sn35.94:10.2: it doesn’t waver at contacts at all.
translation_text.sn35.94:10.3: Mendicants, those who have mastered greed and hate
